items.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting

items.title: Topological Neural Operators
items.summary: We introduce Topological Neural Operators (TNOs), a principled framework for operator learning on cell complexes that lifts neural operators (NOs) from functions on points and/or edges to topological domains. items.title: Bandits for Efficient Experimentation: Adapting to Control Group, Preferences, and Context Drifts
items.summary: We consider a variant of the linear contextual stochastic multi-armed bandits, where the learner must provide recommendations to a group of users, each having its personalized preference vector, and in the presence of context distributions that are drifting over time. items.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality

items.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution

items.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation

items.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
items.summary: Neural machine translation for digitally low-resource Indigenous languages is often hindered by extreme data scarcity, prompting reliance on extractive web-scraping. To ensure data sovereignty, this study introduces a data synthesis methodology to bootstrap NMT models without scraping target-languag

items.title: Preserving Plasticity in Continual Learning via Dynamical Isometry
items.summary: Continual training of deep neural networks under non-stationarity often leads to a progressive loss of plasticity, eventually limiting further learning. We relate plasticity to the empirical Neural Tangent Kernel, and identify dynamical isometry (the condition that layer-wise Jacobian singular value

items.title: Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning
items.summary: Parametric imitation learning via behavior cloning can suffer from poor generalization to out-of-distribution states due to compounding errors during deployment. items.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
items.summary: Foundation models are moving from response generation into operational roles. items.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
items.summary: Existing benchmarks for deep research agents (DRAs) assess only single-shot outputs, ignoring a key question: can DRAs improve their reports when guided by feedback? items.title: Hybrid Robustness Verification for Spatio-Temporal Neural Networks
items.summary: With AI increasingly deployed in safety-critical systems, providing formal robustness guarantees for the underlying models is essential. items.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research
items.summary: Large language models are increasingly expected to handle complex, long-horizon real-world tasks whose context demands can grow without bound, yet model context windows remain inherently finite. items.title: Beyond Probabilistic Similarity: Structural, Temporal, and Causal Limitations of Retrieval-Augmented Generation in the Legal Domain
items.summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a standard architectural response to unreliability in legal AI, yet high-profile failures, including fabricated citations submitted to courts and anachronistic legal content presented as current, continue to appear across jurisdictions. items.title: Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation: A Learned Precursor to Reward Hacking and Its Generalization
items.summary: Reward hacking is usually studied after it becomes visible, once a model earns high proxy reward while failing the intended task. items.title: Learning to Attack and Defend: Adaptive Red Teaming of Language Models via GRPO

items.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). items.title: Weighted universal approximation of differentiable maps on infinite-dimensional manifolds
items.summary: We generalize the universal approximation theorem for functional input neural networks (FNN) to differentiable maps by including the approximation of the derivatives. items.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
items.summary: We present \textbf{Echo-Memory}, a controlled study of memory mechanisms in action-conditioned world models. items.title: Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration: Automating Time-Series Models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum

items.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
items.summary: Nearby neurons in cortex share similar response profiles, producing systematic spatial organization across sensory and cognitive systems. items.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
items.summary: A useful phone agent needs to be personally intelligent. It should reason over a user's identity, history, and preferences as they exist on the device, not just follow isolated instructions in an impersonal sandbox.
